host: i7-11800H (16T) / RTX 3060 Laptop 6 GiB (GA106, CC 8.6) / Win11 / driver 576.83 / CUDA 12.9 / WDDM
clock_state: monitored-boost (I4 deviation; mem clock 6000 stable; interleaved A/B is thermal-robust)

optimization.name: CAMELID_ATTN_COALESCED
optimization.kernel: attn_sk_scores_coalesced (one warp per key position, coalesced half K loads + warp-shuffle reduction)
optimization.applies_to: split-K attention path (attn_shared > 512, i.e. ctx > 512); no-op at ctx<=512
optimization.recommendation: KEEP OPT-IN (default-off). Flipping default-on was tested and REVERTED: it re-associates the split-K decode dot so decode is no longer bit-identical to the spec-verify paths (attention_batched / attention_tree_batched), breaking the lossless-speculative-decode guarantee at ctx>512 (splitk_spec_verify_bit_identical self-skips with it on). Enabling it trades that guarantee for +5-8% long-ctx greedy speed. The default-on-able win must be a bit-identity-preserving KV-read vectorization instead.
optimization.default_on_verdict: REJECTED — breaks lossless spec-decode at ctx>512

correctness.result: byte-identical output_token_ids (baseline vs CAMELID_ATTN_COALESCED=1); both 309 bytes, diff clean

measurements.cache_state: cold prompt cache, warmup run discarded
measurements.trend: speedup scales with context (more KV read -> more benefit); no-op at ctx~0
measurements.ctx0_effect: none (split-K inactive); measured CUDA_GRAPHS=-5% separately confirms ctx~0 is GEMV-bandwidth-bound not launch-bound

recon_context.note: big GEMVs already near-peak; U-unroll bump refuted. Remaining GEMV headroom is small attention projections (occupancy).

cuda_graphs_ctx0.verdict: graphs -5% at ctx~0 (fixed-shape attention tax); Lane A/A2 empty confirmed
